title: Stellaria pentandra
taxonomy: Stellaria pentandra is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. This family of flowering plants is commonly known as the pink or carnation family. Stellaria pentandra belongs to the genus Stellaria, which comprises around 10-12 species of plants. The species is native to mountainous regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The classification of Stellaria pentandra reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants in the Caryophyllaceae family.

intro: Stellaria pentandra, also known as starry campion, is a perennial plant species native to the Arctic tundra. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stellaria pentandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stellaria pentandra is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
